items.title: Setting up my ML environment from scratch: MedMind
items.description:
I decided to build a clinical AI system from scratch. Not using the OpenAI API. Not a tutorial project. An actual system where I train my own model, build my own search pipeline, and deploy the whole thing.

This post is about Day 1 — setting up everything before writing a single line of ML

items.title: Building VoterAssist: An Intent-Driven AI Election Guide
I built an image compressor that never sees your images published
items.description:
Every online image compressor I tried had the same problem: they upload your photos to a server.
TinyPNG, iLoveIMG, Compress2Go — they all work the same way. You pick a file, it goes to someone else's computer, gets compressed, comes back. The compression is good. But your photo — with its GP

items.title: contrast()
items.description: The contrast() filter function increases or decreases the contrast of an element. contrast() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.

items.title: contrast-color()
items.description: The contrast-color() function takes a <color> and returns either black or white, whichever is the most contrasting color for that value. contrast-color() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.
